titleStmt.title: A Transcription of John in Codex Palatinus
VL2
The New Testament
The Gospel according to John
john
titleStmt.funder: The Arts and Humanities Research Council

sponsor.name: The Institute for Textual Scholarship and Electronic Editing

publisher.name: The Institute for Textual Scholarship and Electronic Editing
University of Birmingham

availability.p: Licensed under a Creative Commons Attribution 4.0 International License (CC BY 4.0): available for re-use provided attribution is made to the original creators (the IGNTP and any named participants) and an indication is given of any changes made. See further .

msItem.title: Quattuor Euangelia
Four Gospels in order Lc Mc Mt Jo

msItem.p: The manuscript is lacunose in John 18:12-15

condition.p: The manuscript is lacunose in John 18:12-15

surrogates.bibl: Black and white photographsin ITSEE, supplied by Vetus Latina-Institut.

projectDesc.p: This transcription was made by the Verbum Project as part of an online and printed edition of the Old Latin versions of John between 2003 and 2010.

editorialDecl.p: The transcription covers ff. 43v-118v. It includes foliation, lineation, all punctuation and abbreviations. Digraphs are also recorded. The initial transcription file was a plain text file for use with COLLATE. It was compared with Tischendorf and also with notes made by D. de Bruyne in the INTF copy of this edition in Münster. Further information about the procedures followed may be found in the electronic edition at
This second edition of the file was converted to XML by Catherine Smith using the project's Python conversion scripts 1.0.
The XML specifications used are version 1.5 of the IGNTP Guidelines available at

revisionDesc.change: Adjustment of transcription to version 1.5 of the XML by Hugh Houghton.
Reconversion of the original plain text file using Python scripts to version 1.4 of the XML by Catherine Smith and manual checking.
Hyphen entity replaced by lb attribute break="no" by Hugh Houghton
Changes made in order to display properly with VLI.xsl transformation by Hugh Houghton
Adjustment for IGNTP transcripts display by Hugh Houghton
Correction to John 4:38, and change to verse division in 5:18-19 by Hugh Houghton
Transcription converted to the TEI XML schema and corrected for parsing by Hugh Houghton

ab.note: still in DeB
Tischendorf (and De Bruyne) do not record the punctuation here.
De Bruyne corrects Tischendorf's 'gratia et ueri' to 'gratiae ueri': the image is difficult to read.
sic DeB
'accepimus' may be followed by the abbreviation for 'est' (in place of 'et'?), but this is not recorded by Tischendorf, De Bruyne or Jülicher.
Tischendorf confirms the absence of 'autem' here; Jülicher is in error.
sic DeB
The text of this line has been reconstructed on the basis of Tischendorf (omitting the initial 'et'; confirmed by De Bruyne), given Jülicher's inaccuracy in John 1:17.
sic DeB
sic DeB
sic DeB
sic DeB
sic DeB
De Bruyne records the quire signature XIII in the bottom margin.
Tischendorf's 'auritorrium' is corrected by De Bruyne and Juelicher.
It is possible that 'hoc' has been corrected to 'huc'.
De Bruyne records the quire signature XIIII in the bottom margin.
Our images are unclear. We have followed De Bruyne's correction of Tischendorf ('cit' for 'ut'); Jülicher suggests 'ait ?', but the bow of the 'c' is more probable on the images.
Correction independently confirmed
De Bruyne records the quire signature XV in the bottom margin.
independent of De Bruyne
There is a lacuna in the parchment between 'credit' and 'in'.
Juelicher incorrectly adds 'huius' before 'saeculi'.
Punctuation either side of 'xii' added on authority of De Bruyne.
sic De Bruyne

Between lines 11 and 12 of column 1, the first hand has placed two pairs of dots. These are apparently expunction marks, and may refer to the word 'et' in line 11, under which they sit. Alternatively, given the narrow 'p' of 'peccare', it may be the scribe has misread his own text as 'nolite', and 'corrected' accordingly. This is not noted by De Bruyne.
There is a mark over the 'e' of 'ueritas' which may be a 'u'; nothing is recorded by Tischendorf.
De Bruyne does not alter Tisch 's iudicet
Independently confirmed by De Bruyne
De Bruyne says: [app][*]s[/*][C*]ali[/C*][/app]
Space added following De Bruyne
De Bruyne records the quire signature XVI in the bottom margin.
De Bruyne confirms independently
Spacing confirmed by De Bruyne
As both Tischendorf and Juelicher read hierosolyma, this nasal bar, which appears clearly in the photos, may be an extraneous mark on the image.
Juelicher records a correction of 'sio' to 'scio', not listed in Tischendorf (or supplied by De Bruyne), which is not apparent on our photographs.
First hand reading supplied from Juelicher and De Bruyne (and Tischendorf): our images are hard to read, but the spacing is consonant with an expunction.
De Bruyne records the quire signature XVII in the bottom margin.
Independently confirmed by De Bruyne
The first hand reading has been supplied from De Bruyne, and is consistent with the smaller size letters at the end of the line
DeB conf. indep.
First hand reading supplied on basis of Juelicher and De Bruyne.
The presence of 'illum' was suggested from the images, and independently supported by De Bruyne's correction to Tischendorf.
de B confirms
First hand reading supplied by De Bruyne and confirmed from images.
De Bruyne confirms
de B confirms
Juelicher states that 'no' has been corrected to 'nomine'; this is not recorded by Tischendorf (or De Bruyne) and is no longer visible from the manuscript.
De B confirms
Cross and manuscript note in early mediaeval cursive hand at this point; 'In traditionem symboli'.
De Bruyne records the quire signature XX in the bottom margin.
not first hand correction
Note in darker ink, in early mediaeval cursive, apparently same as on F102v; "finit".
Unusually for this manuscript, the initial two letters of 'tunc' are projecting into the margin.
In place of 'ei', De Bruyne believes the first hand wrote 'et' and it was subsequently deleted. We have followed Jülicher's reading; the photographs are unclear.
De Bruyne records the quire signature XXI in the bottom margin.
Juelicher incorrectly reads 'malagmani' for 'malagmam'.
confirmed by De Bruyne
De Bruyne confirms
De Bruyne confirms
De Bruyne confirms
The final line is followed by a line of decoration.
This is a blank folio.
This is a blank folio.
This is a blank folio.
De Bruyne confirms
The page continues with: INCIPIT CATA LUCAN.
